heading: Extraordinary protective services
label: 4314 Extraordinary protective services

children.heading: General authority
children.label: a General authority
children.text: The Secretary may provide extraordinary protective services for foreign missions directly, by contract, or through State or local authority to the extent deemed necessary by the Secretary in carrying out this chapter, except that the Secretary may not provide under this section any protective services for which authority exists to provide such services under subsections (a)(7) and (d) of .section 3056A of title 18

children.heading: Requirement of extraordinary circumstances
children.label: b Requirement of extraordinary circumstances
children.text: The Secretary may provide funds to a State or local authority for protective services under this section only if the Secretary has determined that a threat of violence, or other circumstances, exists which requires extraordinary security measures which exceed those which local law enforcment agencies can reasonably be expected to take.1So in original. Probably should be “enforcement”.1

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 103–236, title I, § 139(2)108 Stat. 397Apr. 30, 1994
children.label: c Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 103–236, title I, § 139(2)108 Stat. 397Apr. 30, 1994

children.heading: Restrictions on use of funds
children.label: d Restrictions on use of funds

children.text: not more than 20 percent may be obligated for protective services within any single State during that year; and

not less than 15 percent shall be retained as a reserve for protective services provided directly by the Secretary or for expenditures in local jurisdictions not otherwise covered by an agreement for protective services under this section.

children.heading: Period of agreement with State or local authority
children.label: e Period of agreement with State or local authority
children.text: Any agreement with a State or local authority for the provision of protective services under this section shall be for a period of not to exceed 90 days in any calendar year, but such agreements may be renewed after review by the Secretary.

children.heading: Requirement for appropriations
children.label: f Requirement for appropriations
children.text: Contracts may be entered into in carrying out this section only to such extent or in such amounts as are provided in advance in appropriation Acts.

children.heading: Working capital fund
children.label: g Working capital fund
children.text: Amounts used to carry out this section shall not be subject to .section 4308(h) of this title
